member.name: T:KLib.Net.Discovery
member.summary: Provides network discovery utilities for finding available endpoints and addresses.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.Discovery.FindNextAvailableEndPoint
member.summary: Finds the next available TCP endpoint on the best server address and an open port.
member.returns: An representing the next available address and port, or null if no suitable address is found.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.Discovery.FindServerAddress(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Finds the best available IP address for a TCP server. Priority: LAN (192.168.x.x) → Direct Ethernet (169.254.x.x) → localhost. Excludes organisation network (10.10.x.x).
member.param: If true, allows localhost as a fallback address.
member.returns: The best available for server use, or null if none found.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.Discovery.GetDiscoveryAddress(System.Boolean,System.Net.IPAddress)
member.summary: Gets the appropriate discovery address for multicast or broadcast scenarios.
member.param: If true, returns a fixed multicast address.
The base address to determine the broadcast address.
member.returns: The discovery for the given scenario, or null if not applicable.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.Discovery.GetAvailablePort(System.Net.IPAddress)
member.summary: Finds an available TCP port on the specified IP address.
member.param: The IP address to check for an available port.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.DiscoveryBeacon.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.param: Unique server name.
IP address of this server's TCP service.
Port of this server's TCP service.
UDP port to broadcast on. Must match DiscoveryListener.
How often to broadcast the beacon.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.DiscoveryBeacon.Start
member.summary: Start broadcasting beacons.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.DiscoveryBeacon.Stop
member.summary: Stop broadcasting beacons.

member.name: E:KLib.Net.DiscoveryListener.HostDiscovered
member.summary: Raised on the listener thread when a new host is discovered.

member.name: E:KLib.Net.DiscoveryListener.HostDisappeared
member.summary: Raised on the watchdog timer thread when a host has stopped beaconing.

member.name: P:KLib.Net.DiscoveryListener.KnownHosts
member.summary: Returns a snapshot of currently known hosts, keyed by server name. Safe to call from any thread.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.DiscoveryListener.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.param: UDP port to listen on. Must match DiscoveryBeacon.
How long a host can go without beaconing before it is considered gone. Should be several multiples of the beacon interval.
How often the watchdog checks for disappeared hosts.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.DiscoveryListener.Start
member.summary: Start listening for beacons and watching for disappeared hosts.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.DiscoveryListener.Stop
member.summary: Stop listening and clear the known hosts registry.

member.name: T:KLib.Net.KTcpClient
member.summary: Provides TCP client functionality for sending and receiving TcpMessage objects using a length-prefixed wire protocol.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpClient.SendRequest(System.Net.IPEndPoint,KLib.Net.TcpMessage)
member.summary: Sends a TcpMessage request to the specified remote endpoint and returns the response. Handles connection setup and teardown automatically.
member.param: The remote server endpoint to connect to.
The TcpMessage request to send.
member.returns: The TcpMessage response from the server.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpClient.Connect(System.Net.IPEndPoint)
member.summary: Establishes a TCP connection to the specified endpoint.
member.param: The remote endpoint to connect to.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpClient.Close
member.summary: Closes the TCP client and releases associated resources.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpClient.StartBufferedSend(System.Net.IPEndPoint)
member.summary: Starts a buffered send session by connecting to the specified endpoint and initializing stream readers/writers.
member.param: The remote endpoint to connect to.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpClient.EndBufferedSend
member.summary: Ends the buffered send session and closes all associated streams and the client.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpClient.SendRequest(KLib.Net.TcpMessage)
member.summary: Sends a TcpMessage request and blocks until a TcpMessage response arrives. This is the single method all new client code should use.
member.param: The TcpMessage request to send.
member.returns: The TcpMessage response from the server.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpClient.WriteBuffer(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Writes a byte array to the network stream, prefixed with its length as a 32-bit integer.
member.param: The byte array to send.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpClient.ReadBufferedSendResponse
member.summary: Reads a buffered TcpMessage response from the network stream.
member.returns: The TcpMessage response received from the server.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpClient.SendRequestReceiveResponse(System.String)
member.summary: Low-level: sends a JSON string, receives a JSON string back. Sits directly on top of your existing length-prefix wire protocol.
member.param: The JSON string to send.
member.returns: The JSON string response received from the server.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpClient.WriteStringAsByteArray(System.IO.BinaryWriter,System.String)
member.summary: Writes a UTF-8 encoded string to the network stream, prefixed with its length as a 32-bit integer.
member.param: The BinaryWriter to use.
The string message to write.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpClient.ProcessInt32(System.Int32)
member.summary: Processes a 32-bit integer, optionally reversing byte order if needed.
member.param: The raw integer value.

member.name: T:KLib.Net.KTcpListener
member.summary: Provides TCP server functionality for accepting client connections and exchanging TcpMessage objects.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpListener.StartListener(System.Net.IPEndPoint)
member.summary: Starts listening for incoming TCP connections on the specified endpoint.
member.param: The IP endpoint to listen on.
member.returns: True if the listener started successfully.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpListener.CloseListener
member.summary: Stops the TCP listener and releases associated resources.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpListener.Pending
member.summary: Checks if there are pending client connection requests.
member.returns: True if a client is waiting to connect; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpListener.AcceptTcpClient
member.summary: Accepts a pending TCP client connection and initializes the network stream and readers/writers.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpListener.CloseTcpClient
member.summary: Closes the current TCP client connection and disposes the network stream.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpListener.ReadRequest
member.summary: Reads the next incoming message from the stream and deserializes it as a TcpMessage.
member.returns: The deserialized TcpMessage received from the client.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpListener.WriteResponse(KLib.Net.TcpMessage)
member.summary: Serializes a TcpMessage and writes it back to the client.
member.param: The TcpMessage to send to the client.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpListener.ReadString
member.summary: Reads a UTF-8 encoded string from the network stream. The string is prefixed with its length as a 32-bit integer.
member.returns: The string read from the stream.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpListener.WriteStringAsByteArray(System.String)
member.summary: Writes a UTF-8 encoded string to the network stream, prefixed with its length as a 32-bit integer.
member.param: The string to write.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.KTcpListener.ReadBytes
member.summary: Reads a byte array from the network stream, prefixed with its length as a 32-bit integer. Sends an acknowledgement (int value 1) after reading.
member.returns: The byte array read from the stream.

member.name: P:KLib.Net.ServerBeacon.Name
member.summary: Unique name identifying this server (e.g. "SERVER_A").

member.name: P:KLib.Net.ServerBeacon.Address
member.summary: IP address of the server's TCP service.

member.name: P:KLib.Net.ServerBeacon.TcpPort
member.summary: Port of the server's TCP service.

member.name: P:KLib.Net.ServerBeacon.Version
member.summary: Protocol version. Increment if the beacon format changes.

member.name: T:KLib.Net.TcpMessage
member.summary: Unified message envelope used for all TCP communication. Both requests (client→server) and responses (server→client) use this shape.

member.name: P:KLib.Net.TcpMessage.Code
member.summary: Status code. Follows HTTP conventions: 200 = OK 400 = Bad request (client error) 404 = Command not recognised 500 = Server error On outgoing requests from the client, Code can be left 0.

member.name: P:KLib.Net.TcpMessage.Command
member.summary: The command name on a request (e.g. "GetHostName", "Ping"). On a response, used optionally for a human-readable status message.

member.name: P:KLib.Net.TcpMessage.Payload
member.summary: Always a JSON string. Use "{}" when there is no meaningful payload. Never null — the receiver can always safely deserialise this.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.TcpMessage.Ok(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a 200-OK response with an optional JSON payload.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.TcpMessage.Ok(System.Object)
member.summary: Creates a 200-OK response by serialising any object as the payload.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.TcpMessage.BadRequest(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a 400 Bad Request response.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.TcpMessage.NotFound(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a 404 Not Found response (unrecognised command).

member.name: M:KLib.Net.TcpMessage.Error(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a 500 Internal Server Error response.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.TcpMessage.Request(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a command-only request with no payload. The most concise way to send an instruction that requires no accompanying data.

member.name: M:KLib.Net.TcpMessage.GetPayload``1
member.summary: Deserialises the Payload JSON string into a typed object. Usage: var result = response.GetPayload<MyType>();
